content.full_text:
April 11, 1975


Mr. Ralph D. Stern
Schools Attorney
San Diego City Schools
4100 Normal Street
San Diego, California 92103

Dear Mr. Stern:

Re: Request for Opinion
    No. 75-90

Thank you for your letter of March 27, 1975 re-
questing an opinion with respect to the duties of the
Board of Education, San Diego City Schools under the
Political Reform Act.

Since your request raises no substantial question
involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act,
your request for a formal opinion is denied.

Your first question deals with the payment to em-
ployees for the purpose of attending meetings which have
the "purpose of convincing the association to take a
position on pending legislation."

It is reasonable to assume that the taking of a
position on legislation will result in direct communi-
cation with designated officials. Section 82045 defines
reportable payments and paragraph (e) thereof clearly
covers the situation you describe, particularly if it
is read in conjunction with the preceding paragraph (d).

Your second question deals with the paid time of
your employees in dealings with lobbyists for the pur-
pose of having lobbyist organizations take a position
on legislation.

Mr. Ralph D. Stern
April 11, 1975
Page Two


     This is not substantially different from the first
situation you describe and these costs would be report-
able for the same reasons.

     I might add that the Commission has regulations
under consideration which may, in the future and under
some circumstances, obviate your need to report pay-
ments similar in nature to the ones you describe above.

     Although no formal opinion will be issued, I hope
the foregoing answers your questions and will be suf-
ficiently helpful. If you have further questions, I
urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman

DHL:cjb

BOARD OF EDUCATION
SAN DIEGO CITY SCHOOLS

March 27, 1975

The Honorable Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

Pursuant to Government Code Section 83114 an opinion is
hereby requested on whether or not the following activities
are to be reported under Proposition 9:

1. Attendance at meetings of associations (or their
   committees or subcommittees) such as the
   Association of California School Administrators,
   the California Association of School Business
   Officials, the California Congress of Parents
   and Teachers, and the like where an employee
   of the school district is attending on school
   district time for the purpose of convincing
   the association to take a position on pending
   legislation; and

2. Contacts by school district employees with
   lobbyists for various organizations such as
   those named above for the purpose of persuading
   them to take a position on pending legislation
   on behalf of the organization they represent.

If I can provide any additional information, please do not
hesitate to contact me at your convenience.

Sincerely,

RALPH D. STERN
Schools Attorney

RDS:dbg

cc: Members of the Board of Education and the Superintendent

Education Center, 4100 Normal Street, San Diego, California 92103 • Telephone (714) 293-4681

Since your request raises no substantial question involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act, your request for a formal opinion is denied. Your first question deals with the payment to employees for the purpose of attending meetings which have the "purpose of convincing the association to take a position on pending legislation." It is reasonable to assume that the taking of a position on legislation will result in direct communication with designated officials. Section 82045 defines reportable payments and paragraph (e) thereof clearly covers the situation you describe, particularly if it is read in conjunction with the preceding paragraph (d).
